name: Termez
description: Termez is a 2,500-year-old strategic city on the Amu Darya near Afghanistan — a logistics and border hub where ancient Bactria, Buddhist sites and Islamic scholarship meet. Surkhandarya’s hot climate and ancient fortresses make it a distinctive destination. Old Termez (6th c. BC–17th c. AD) is a state reserve and museum since 2019; the region is a centre for Islamic and Buddhist studies.

monuments.title: Hakim at-Termezi complex
monuments.info: 9th–15th c. shrine over Sufi scholar Hakim at-Termizi; mausoleum, mosque, khanaka and chillakhana; restored in Timurid times; on Old Termez citadel by the river.

monuments.title: Sultan Saodat complex
monuments.info: 10th–17th c. sacred cemetery of Prophet Muhammad’s descendants (sayyids) around Hasan al-Amir’s tomb; rebuilt 2002/2019; distinctive brickwork.

monuments.title: Fayaztepa Buddhist monastery
monuments.info: 1st–7th c. AD vihara near Termez; found 1963; prayer hall, courtyard and service rooms; clay Buddha and Triad seal.

monuments.title: Kirk Kiz fortress
monuments.info: 9th–15th c. mud-brick quadrangle — noble residence, khanaka or caravanserai; legend of heroine Guloyim and forty companions.

monuments.title: Kampyrtepa site
monuments.info: 4th c. BC Bactrian site 30 km west on the Amu Darya, often linked to Alexandria Oxiana; palace walls and dwellings.

monuments.title: Zurmala stupa
monuments.info: 2nd c. BC Kushan Buddhist stupa ~14.5 m high of ~1.2 million bricks — among Central Asia’s earliest brick monuments.

monuments.title: Abu Isa Muhammad at-Termezi mausoleum
monuments.info: Shrine of Imam al-Tirmidhi, author of Jami‘ at-Tirmidhi, in Sherabad District; white marble and carved ganch.

monuments.title: Jarqurghan minaret
monuments.info: 1109 minaret in Jarqurghan District (~60 km from Termez) by Ali ibn Muhammad al-Sarakhsi; spiral brickwork; ~21.6 m of the lower tier remains.

monuments.title: Qoratepa complex
monuments.info: 1st–3rd c. AD Kushan cave monasteries northeast of Old Termez on three hills — major Buddhist centre in Central Asia.
